prompt: Six validated/testable claims from Phello substrate geometry research (Sprint 0-2). Build a research agenda that extends these into a publishable, fundable program.

thesis.current: Three-track DAG research agenda. Track 1 (Context Geometry): Claims 1-2 + CKA relational justification + cosine baseline + debiased CKA + Qwen replication. Track 2 (Coherence Thermometer): Claim 4 + CKA base rate + dense-vs-MoE + Qwen scaling ladder + expected sensitivity ordering (D1>D3>D2). Track 3 (Sprint 2.5 fork): Path A (germaneness) or Path B (coherence-preference tradeoff). Claim 6 = future work (ICLR 2026 singular vector rotation motivates). Research produces measurement framework; Phello applies as product input.

supporting: Claims 1-2 validated with clean Sprint 0-1 data
CKA justified for cross-input relational comparison (cosine-equivalence doesn't apply)
Coherence-control tradeoff documented in RepE — Sprint 2 connects
ICLR 2026: singular vector rotation correlates with OOD degradation
Qwen scaling ladder enables cheap generalization test
Both Sprint 2.5 fork paths lead to publishable outcomes
Research-product tension dissolved via measurement-input framing
No competitors doing geometric preference measurement
Expected CKA sensitivity ordering helps interpret Sprint 2.5
challenging: CKA outlier sensitivity — debiased CKA comparison needed
CKA insensitive to local structure — D2 may not separate
Sprint 2.5 hasn't run — Path A/B reasoning is hypothetical
Generalization to >14B may require collaborator compute
RLVR spurious rewards complicate future Claim 6 validation
